LAW DEPARTMENT.HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE

ESTABLISHED 1832.

Chief Justice—Sir William Comer Petheram, Kt. Q.C.Puisne Judges—Sir Henry T. Prinsep, J. F. Norris, Q.C., J. Q. Pigot, B.A., James O'Kinealy, W. Macpherson, E. J. Trevelyan, Chunder Madhub Ghose, H. Beverley, M.A., Dr. Gooroo Das Banerjee, Amir Ali, O.I.E., C. H. Hill, and R. F. Rampini.LAW OFFICERS.Advocate General—Sir Gregory C. Paul, K.C.I.E., B.A.Standing Counsel—A. PhillipsSolicitor—R. L. UptonSupt. and Remembrancer—C. A. WilkinsDeputy dr.—G. C. KilbyChief Law Reporter—J. V. WoodmanLaw Reporters—H. T. Hyde, C. D. PaniotySenior Govt. Pleader—Hem Chunder BanerjeeOFFICERS OF THE COURT.Taxing Officer, Registrar (Original Jurisdiction), Keeper of Records, and Accountant Gen.—R. BelchambersFirst Asst. or Dy. Registrar—W. R. FinkSecond Asst. Registrar—Greeschunder BonnerjeeThird Asst. Registrar—J. H. HeckleFourth Asst. Registrar—Kailas Chandra BhattacharyaRegistrar and Taxing Officer, Appellate Jurisdiction—H. W. C. CarnduffDeputy Registrar do—W. H. JoyceAsst. Registrar, Appellate Jurisdiction—J. LewisClerk of the Crown—J. G. ApcarCoroner of Calcutta—Dr. E. W. ChambersReceiver—J. C. MacgregorOfficial Trustee & Assignee—A. B. MillerAdministrator General—L. P. D. Broughton—on furl.Private Secretary and Clerk to the Chief Justice—T. J. WaiteADVOCATES OF THE HIGH COURT.

SURVEY AND TELEGRAPH DEPARTMENTS

The names of the officers in the Telegraph and Survey Departments serving in the Punjab will be found serving under "India."

POLITICAL DEPARTMENT.AGENTS TO THE LIEUTENANT-GOVERNOR.

The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Patandi
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Dujana
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Loharu
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Maler Kotla
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Delhi Division for the time being, for Kalsia and the Hill States under Superintendent, Hill States
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Jullundur Division for the time being, for Kapurthalla
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Jullundur Division for the time being, for Mandi
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Jullundur Division for the time being, for Suket
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Jullundur Division for the time being, for Faridkot
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Lahore Division for the time being, for Chamba
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Pesháwar Division for the time being, for Frontier Tribes bordering on the districts of his Division
The Commissioner and Superintendent of the Derájat Division for the time being, for Frontier Tribes bordering on the districts of his Division

HILL STATES.

Comprising Simur (Nahan), Kahlu (Bilaspur), Bashahr, Hindur (Nálagarh), Keonthal, Baghal, Baghat Jubbol, Kumharsain, Bhajji, Mailog, Balean, Dhami, Kuthar, Kunhiar, Mangal, Bija Darkoti, Trahoch and Sängri. The Deputy Commissioner of Simla is ex-officio Superintendent.

FRONTIER TRIBES.

The Deputy Commissioners of Hazára, Pesháwur, Kohát, Bannu, Dera Ismail Khan and Dera Ghazi Khan are Political Officers with respect to the Independent Tribes bordering on their respective Districts

Political Officer—Lt. Col. R. Warburton, C.S.I., S.C.

NOTE.—By the Madras and Bombay Armies Act, 1893 (56 & 57 Vict. cap. 62) the offices of Comr.-in-Chief and of Sec. to Govt. in the Mily. Dept. of those Presidencies are to be abolished. This Act will take effect from 1st April, 1895.

BRITISH CAVALRY.19th (Princess of Wales's Own) Hussars.

The Elephant.
"Mysore"—"Assaye"—"Niagara."—"Egypt, 1882, 1884"—"Tel-el-Kebir"—"Nile, 1884-85"—
"Abu Klea."
Uniform, Blue. Busby-bag and Plume, White.

Arrived in India 25th September 1891.—Bangalore.

Depôt at Canterbury.

BRITISH INFANTRY.
The Royal Scots (Lothian Regiment.)
(Late 1st Foot.)

SECOND BATTALION.

The Collar and Badge of the Order of the Thistle, with the Royal Cypher. The Thistle ensigned with the Imperial Crown. The Sphinx superscribed "Egypt."

"Blenheim"—"Ramillies"—"Oudenarde"—"Malplaquet"—"Louisburg"—"St. Lucia"—
"Egmont-op-Zee"—"Corunna"—"Busaco"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—
"St. Sebastian"—"Nive"—"Peninsula"—"Niagara"—"Waterloo"—
"Nagpore"—"Maheidpore"—"Ava"—"Alma"—"Inkerman"—
"Sevastopol"—"Taku Forts"—"Pekin."

Uniform, Scarlet. Facings, Blue.

Arrived in India 24th March 1892.—Belgaum.

Regimental District No. 1—Glencorse.

Rank and Names. Regimental Rank. Army Rank. Remarks.

Raymond, H. P. 11 July 77 G 1 Oct. 77 Europe—Retired

BRITISH INFANTRY.The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regiment.
(Late 2nd Foot.)FIRST BATTALION."Pristinae virtutis memor"—"Vel exuviae triumphant."

The Paschal Lamb. The Royal Cypher within the Garter. The Sphinx.

"Egypt"—"Vimiera"—"Corunna"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—"Pyrenees"—"Nivelle"—"Toulouse"

“Carnatic”—“Sholinghur”—“Mysore”—“Seringapatam, 1799”—“Egypt (with the Sphinx), 1800-2”—
“Assaye, 1803”—“Java, 1811”—“Nagpore, 1817”—“Mahidpore, 1817”—“Ava, 1824-6”—“Meeanee and
Hyderabad, 1843”—“Pegu”—“Persia”—“Lucknow, 1857”—“Central India, 1857”—“Taku
Forts”—“Pekin”—“Abyssinia”—“Afghanistan, 1878-80”—“Egypt, 1882”—
“Tel-el-Kebir, 1882”—“Suakin, 1885”—“Tofrek”—
“Burma, 1885-87.”

BENGAL RETIRED OFFICERS.ARRANGED ALPHABETICALLY.

(The names of General Officers of the Local Service retired under the Royal Warrant of 31st Dec. 1877, and of those of the Staff Corps and Royal (formerly Indian) Artillery and Engineers retired under the Royal Warrants of the 13th August, 1877, 1st May, 1887, and 25th June, 1881, will be found at pp. 192, 199, 204 and 205.)

BOMBAY RETIRED OFFICERS.

(The names of General Officers of the Local Service retired under the Royal Warrant of 31st December, 1877, and of those of the Staff Corps and Royal (late Indian) Artillery and Engineers retired under the Royal Warrants of 13th August, 1877, 1st May 1878, and 25th June, 1881, will be found at pages 192, 199, 204, and 205.)

INDEX.—ACTIVE SERVICE.

NOTE.—Native names will be found under the first portion of the name, omitting the following words when used as titles:—Babu, Bakhshi, Haji, Jemadar, Kazi, Khan Bahadur, Khan Sahib, Khwajah, Maharajah, Maulvi, Mir, Mirza, Nawab, Pundit, Rai, Rai Bahadur, Rajah, Rao, Rao Bahadur, Rao Saheb, Seth, Shahzadah, Shaikh, Sirdar, Subadar, Syud, and Wazirzadah.
